op.name: select
op.notes: Return array from list of choices based on conditions. Cost: numel(input).
op.summary: Return an array drawn from elements in choicelist, depending on conditions.

blocks.text: Return an array drawn from elements in choicelist, depending on conditions.

desc_blocks.text:
The list of conditions which determine from which array in `choicelist`
the output elements are taken. When multiple conditions are satisfied,
the first one encountered in `condlist` is used.

The list of arrays from which the output elements are taken. It has
to be of the same length as `condlist`.
The element inserted in `output` when all conditions evaluate to False.

The output at position m is the m-th element of the array in
`choicelist` where the m-th element of the corresponding array in
`condlist` is True.

blocks.text:
Beginning with an array of integers from 0 to 5 (inclusive),
elements less than ``3`` are negated, elements greater than ``3``
are squared, and elements not meeting either of these conditions
(exactly ``3``) are replaced with a `default` value of ``42``.

blocks.code:
>>> x = np.arange(6)
>>> condlist = [x<3, x>3]
>>> choicelist = [x, x**2]
>>> np.select(condlist, choicelist, 42)
array([ 0,  1,  2, 42, 16, 25])

blocks.text:
When multiple conditions are satisfied, the first one encountered in
`condlist` is used.

blocks.code:
>>> condlist = [x<=4, x>3]
>>> choicelist = [x, x**2]
>>> np.select(condlist, choicelist, 55)
array([ 0,  1,  2,  3,  4, 25])

blocks.text: where — Return elements from one of two arrays depending on condition.
